chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Crl. Bail Application No.S- 450 of 2023 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge. 1.For orders on office objections 2.For hearing of Bail Application. Mr. J. K. Jarwar, Advocate along with applicant. Mr. Shafi Muhammad Mahar, Deputy P.G for the State. Date of Hearing & Order: 09-10-2023 O R D E R MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO, J.- Complainant in FIR has alleged that his brother, namely, Amir was killed by co-accused Sultan Makrani Baloch by causing him a dagger below on his back on 07.05.2023 at 08:15 p.m near Bus Stop adjacent to Makkah Hotel, Kandiaro; whereas, applicant armed with a dagger was in his company and facilitating him. However, subsequently, complainant reportedly filed an affidavit before the trial Court stating that his brother was murdered by applicant and not co-accused Sultan. On the basis of which, main accused Sultan has been granted post-arrest bail by the trial Court. Then, complainant filed an affidavit in this bail application extending no objection to the confirmation of bail to applicant. 2. Prima facie, it appears that complainant has been changing his version of the incident, sometime implicating applicant and sometime implicating co-accused Sultan for murdering his brother. The version narrated by him in the FIR, however, is that not applicant but co- accused Sultan had murdered his brother, who has already been granted post-arrest bail. Therefore, learned Deputy P.G has recorded no objection. 2 3. Accordingly, this bail application is allowed, in the given facts and circumstances, and the interim pre-arrest bail earlier granted to the applicant by this Court is confirmed on the same terms and conditions. 4.

Therefore, learned Deputy P.G has recorded no objection. 2 3. Accordingly, this bail application is allowed, in the given facts and circumstances, and the interim pre-arrest bail earlier granted to the applicant by this Court is confirmed on the same terms and conditions. 4. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and shall not influence the trial Court while deciding the case on merits. JUDGE Ahmad
